title: Dubai's VARA issues specialised regulations for Virtual Assets
summary: On 7 February 2023, VARA -- the Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority, which regulates virtual asset activity across the Emirate of Dubai, including its free zones, but excluding the Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC) -- issued its Virtual Assets and Related Activities Regulations 2023, establishing Dubai's Virtual Asset (VA) Framework. The framework licenses seven VA activities -- advisory, broker-dealer, custodial, exchange, lending-borrowing, payments and remittances, and VA management and investment services -- plus token issuance, and mandates AML standards VARA describes as gold-standard. VARA's announcement says existing MVP Operational, Provisional and Preparatory licence-holders, legacy virtual-asset businesses, and new entrants will be routed toward full FMP licensing, and that firms offering virtual-asset services in Dubai before this publication must register with VARA and become fully compliant, with non-compliance subject to regulatory action.
why_it_matters: VARA is the regulator that licenses and supervises virtual-asset businesses operating in Dubai outside the DIFC free zone, and this framework is what first set out which crypto-related activities -- from exchanges to custody to token issuance -- require a VARA licence there.

citations.quote: will be required to register with VARA and become fully compliant with the final rules
